title: MER2026-Prefer
description: MER-Prefer is a newly introduced track that predicts which of two emotion descriptions is preferred by human annotators for a given video. The concept of emotion preference was first proposed in EmoPrefer, playing a crucial role in training reward models capable of understanding human emotions. We employ the weighted F1-score (WAF) as the primary metric and accuracy (ACC) as the secondary metric. This is part of the MER2026 Challenge at ACM Multimedia 2026.
description_zh: MER-Prefer是一个新推出的赛道，用于预测人类标注者对给定视频的两个情感描述中哪一个更偏好。情感偏好的概念最初在EmoPrefer中提出，在训练能够理解人类情感的奖励模型中起着至关重要的作用。我们采用加权F1分数（WAF）作为主要指标，准确率（ACC）作为次要指标。这是ACM Multimedia 2026的MER2026挑战赛的一部分。

pages.title: data
pages.content:
Data

Please complete the End User License Agreement (EULA) on Hugging Face to access the dataset:

https://huggingface.co/datasets/MERChallenge/MER2026

The EULA clearly states that the dataset is for academic research purposes only and prohibits any modifications or uploads to the Internet. We will review the submitted EULA promptly; once approved, participants will gain access to the dataset.

The MER2026 baseline paper and code are also available:

  • Baseline paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.19417
  • Baseline code (Tracks 1-3): https://github.com/zeroQiaoba/MERTools

Official website: https://zeroqiaoba.github.io/MER-Challenge/
pages.content_zh:
数据

请在Hugging Face上完成最终用户许可协议(EULA)以访问数据集：

https://huggingface.co/datasets/MERChallenge/MER2026

EULA明确声明该数据集仅供学术研究使用，禁止进行任何修改或上传到互联网。我们将及时审核提交的EULA；审核通过后，参赛者将获得数据集的访问权限。

MER2026基准论文和代码也已发布：

  • 基准论文：https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.19417
  • 基准代码(赛道1-3)：https://github.com/zeroQiaoba/MERTools

官方网站：https://zeroqiaoba.github.io/MER-Challenge/

pages.title: overview
pages.content:
MER-Prefer: Emotion Preference

MER-Prefer is a newly introduced track. The concept of emotion preference was first proposed in EmoPrefer, which predicts which of two emotion descriptions is preferred by human annotators for a given video. This task plays a crucial role in training reward models capable of understanding human emotions.

We provide two training datasets: EmoPrefer-Data with majority-voted labels and EmoPrefer-Data-V2 with single-annotator labels. For testing, we provide 379 samples with majority-voted labels.

See the baseline paper for more details: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.19417

Official website: https://zeroqiaoba.github.io/MER-Challenge/

Submission Format

The result file must be named answer.csv with the following columns:

  • name: sample identifier
  • a1: first emotion description (text)
  • a2: second emotion description (text)
  • preference: the preferred description — must be exactly a1 or a2

Compress answer.csv into answer.zip and submit via Codabench.
pages.content_zh: # MER-Prefer：情感偏好 MER-Prefer是一个新引入的赛道。情感偏好的概念首次在EmoPrefer中提出，用于预测人类标注者对给定视频的两个情感描述中哪一个更偏好。该任务在训练能够理解人类情感的奖励模型中起着至关重要的作用。 我们提供两个训练数据集：**EmoPrefer-Data**（带多数投票标签）和**EmoPrefer-Data-V2**（带单个标注者标签）。对于测试，我们提供379个带多数投票标签的样本。 更多详情见基线论文：https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.19417 官方网站：https://zeroqiaoba.github.io/MER-Challenge/ ## 提交格式 结果文件必须命名为 `answer.csv`，包含以下列： - `name`：样本标识符 - `a1`：第一个情感描述（文本） - `a2`：第二个情感描述（文本） - `preference`：偏好的描述——必须恰好是 `a1` 或 `a2` 将 `answer.csv` 压缩成 `answer.zip` 并通过Codabench提交。

pages.title: evaluation
pages.content:
Evaluation

In MER-Prefer, we evaluate two-class preference classification using Weighted F1-score (WAF) as the primary metric and Accuracy (ACC) as the secondary metric.

For each sample, the submission must predict whether description a1 or a2 is preferred by human annotators. The prediction is evaluated against the ground truth preference labels.

The submission must contain exactly 10,000 samples. Only samples whose (name, a1, a2) tuple exists in the ground truth set are evaluated.

See the baseline paper for more details: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.19417

Official website: https://zeroqiaoba.github.io/MER-Challenge/
pages.content_zh:
评估

在MER-Prefer中，我们使用加权F1分数（WAF）作为主要评估指标，准确率（ACC）作为次要评估指标来评估二分类偏好分类。

对于每个样本，提交内容必须预测人类标注员更倾向于描述a1还是a2。预测结果将与真实的偏好标签进行比较。

提交内容必须包含恰好10,000个样本。仅评估其(name, a1, a2)元组存在于真实数据集中的样本。

详见基准论文：https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.19417

官方网站：https://zeroqiaoba.github.io/MER-Challenge/
